assertion.text: Erdős Problem #604: declared status 'open'. Formalized: no. Given $n$ distinct points $A\subset\mathbb{R}^2$ must there be a point $x\in A$ such that\[\#\{ d(x,y) : y \in A\} \gg n^{1-o(1)}?\]Or even $\gg n/\sqrt{\log n}$? Current best: The best known bound is\[\gg n^{c-o(1)},\]due to Katz and Tardos \cite{KaTa04}, where\[c=\frac{48-14e}{55-16e}=0.864137\cdots.\]  References  [Er75f] Erd\H{o}s, Paul, On some problems of elementary and combinatorial geometry. Prize: $500. Tags: distances, geometry.
